claims.claim: The pipeline is stories/bugfix/ and its README is the authoritative reference for rooms, exits, judge modes, cycle budgets, and the RED→GREEN regression-gate discipline
claims.note: stories/bugfix/README.md exists and documents all of these: rooms table, exits table, judge polymorphism, cycle budgets (Wave 3/Phase 4), and a 'RED→GREEN regression gate' section that explicitly names the characterization-test trap.

claims.claim: Full pipeline via `start` / `full_pipeline` walks idle → reproducing → proposing → implementing → testing → reviewing → validating → done
claims.note: bugfix README intent table lists both `start` and `full_pipeline` landing at reproducing_executing; room files for all eight rooms exist under stories/bugfix/rooms/.

claims.claim: Triage-only mode via the `triage` intent or `bugfix_mode=triage` gives a read-only ALREADY-FIXED/STILL-LIVE/PARTIAL/UNCLEAR verdict with no worktree
claims.note: rooms/idle.yaml defines a `triage` intent that sets bugfix_mode=triage and targets `triaging`; idle's workspace-creation effects are all guarded on bugfix_mode != 'triage' and triage mode pins workdir='.'; verdict vocabulary (ALREADY-FIXED, STILL-LIVE) appears in triage flows.

claims.claim: stories/bugfix/rooms/triaging.yaml exists
claims.note: File exists at that path.

claims.claim: Quick fix (`quick_fix` intent) skips the reviewing + validating checkpoints; see README#mode-shortcuts
claims.note: README has a 'Mode shortcuts' section and the intent table says quick mode's accept at testing_awaiting_reply jumps to done_executing, skipping reviewing + validating.

claims.claim: The pipeline ends via the `bugfix_exit` world key: `direct-ship` (default, composes the shared ship-it tail integrate → verify → cleanup) or `open-PR` (hands off to pr-refinement)
claims.note: app.yaml:328 declares bugfix_exit with default "direct-ship"; README's exit-slot section describes the imported ship-it tail (integrate → verify → cleanup) and the open-PR handoff; stories/ship-it/README.md and stories/pr-refinement/README.md both exist.

claims.claim: An opt-in `open-PR-merge` exit walks pr-refinement's CI-watch and merge to a real @exit:merged with no parent hub; see README#the-exit-slot--direct-ship-vs-open-pr-delivery-loop-slice-4
claims.note: README heading 'The exit slot — direct-ship vs open-PR (delivery-loop slice 4)' matches the anchor; it documents open-PR-merge composing imported pr-refinement (open_pr → ci_monitoring → merge) reaching @exit:merged standalone.

claims.claim: A standalone run selects the exit via `initial_world: {bugfix_exit: open-PR}`; dev-story instead derives it from its own `bugfix_destination` choice
claims.note: bugfix_exit is a plain world var (app.yaml world block), overridable via initial_world; stories/dev-story/app.yaml:505 declares bugfix_destination and line 1667 maps it to bugfix_exit (local-merge→direct-ship, else open-PR).

claims.claim: Prerequisite: repo must be onboarded per ../project-onboarding.md so a ticket source (local issues/bugs/ or a bound host.gh.ticket GitHub repo) exists
claims.note: docs/project-onboarding.md exists and covers both local-file tickets under issues/ and binding iface.ticket → host.gh.ticket to a GitHub repo, plus the agent-backend prerequisite.

claims.claim: The repro_command/gate_command convention (ticket frontmatter repro_command seeds world.gate_command) is documented in stories/bugfix/README.md
claims.note: README's 'repro RED-gate' section documents exactly this: ticket `repro_command:` frontmatter projected into world.gate_command at ticket-load.

claims.claim: TUI runs from dev-story with judge_mode=human as the standalone default, so each checkpoint pauses for an explicit bf__accept instead of auto-cascading under judge_mode=llm
claims.note: bugfix app.yaml/README declare judge_mode default `human`; the judge polymorphism section confirms llm mode auto-fires verdicts while human mode waits for explicit reply intents.

claims.claim: Start the TUI with bare `kitsoki run`
claims.note: cmd/kitsoki/main.go `run` accepts MaximumNArgs(1); with no arg it synthesizes the implicit project root from .kitsoki.yaml (a dev-story instance), so the no-arg form is valid.

claims.claim: TUI command sequence: tickets → search_tickets open → pick_ticket TKT-100 → go_bugfix → bf__start → bf__proceed/bf__accept walk through reproducing, proposing, implementing, testing, reviewing, validating, done → @exit:done → pr.open_pr
claims.note: Matches stories/dev-story/README.md 'Manual TUI walkthrough' (lines 510–528) verbatim, including the exact bf__proceed/bf__accept ordering and the bf @exit:done → pr.open_pr handoff.

claims.claim: `go_bugfix` forces bf regardless of ticket_type; `drive` also routes here when ticket_type is "bug"
claims.note: dev-story README: the walkthrough types go_bugfix for a bug; `drive` reads ticket_type and routes accordingly (impl for feature tickets).

claims.claim: For triage-only: after go_bugfix lands at bf.idle, type `bf__triage` instead of `bf__start` — lands at a read-only verdict, no worktree
claims.note: The `triage` intent is declared on bugfix's idle room and targets the triaging room with no workspace creation; under the bf import alias intents are prefixed bf__ (same rewriter mechanism as bf__start/bf__accept per README's IntentAliases note).

claims.claim: See stories/dev-story/README.md#manual-tui-walkthrough for the full walkthrough including the feature-ticket (drive → impl) branch
claims.note: That heading exists at stories/dev-story/README.md:505 and the drive→impl feature branch is described right after the walkthrough.

claims.claim: Standalone: `kitsoki run stories/bugfix/app.yaml`
claims.note: stories/bugfix/app.yaml exists; the bugfix README gives the identical standalone command.

claims.claim: Verify command: `go run ./cmd/kitsoki test flows stories/bugfix/app.yaml --flows 'stories/bugfix/flows/triage_verdict_autonomous.yaml'`
claims.note: `kitsoki test flows <app.yaml>` exists (cmd/kitsoki/test_flows.go) with a --flows override flag, and stories/bugfix/flows/triage_verdict_autonomous.yaml exists.

claims.claim: Verify command: `go run ./cmd/kitsoki test flows stories/bugfix/app.yaml --flows 'stories/bugfix/flows/done_opens_pr_and_merges.yaml'`
claims.note: stories/bugfix/flows/done_opens_pr_and_merges.yaml exists; same command shape as above.

claims.claim: The full flow suite covers the pipeline end-to-end — run with --flows 'stories/bugfix/flows/*.yaml'; the README has the index
claims.note: 80+ flow fixtures exist under stories/bugfix/flows/ and the README's file-layout section indexes them.

claims.claim: The kitsoki-debugging skill (.agents/skills/kitsoki-debugging/SKILL.md) diagnoses the state machine against on-disk repo state without a live session
claims.note: .agents/skills/kitsoki-debugging/SKILL.md exists; the dev-workflow-matrix's Fix-a-bug×TUI note independently corroborates that silent-bounce reliability warranted this dedicated debugging skill.

claims.claim: Web: `kitsoki web` runs the same bugfix orchestrator over HTTP but is reliability-suspect (staged-gate and pacing issues historically)
claims.note: kitsoki web is a real subcommand (listed in cmd/kitsoki/doctor.go command groups); the matrix's Fix-a-bug×Web row says exactly 'drivable; staged-gate & pace issues historically' — 🟡 proof-thin.

claims.claim: VS Code: drivable via the same webview embed as file-a-bug.md; a dedicated e2e spec tools/vscode-kitsoki/tests/vscode-bugfix-walk.e2e.spec.ts (real VS Code via kitsoki.flow) drives the full pipeline idle → done → @exit:done
claims.note: docs/workflows/file-a-bug.md exists; the spec file exists and its header + code confirm the idle → …cascade… → done → @exit:done walk in a real VS Code window via the extension's `kitsoki.flow` setting.

claims.claim: The VS Code spec is the real socket capture that pinned host.ide.get_diagnostics' wire shape (still-open follow-up; no standalone IDE-integration architecture doc yet)
claims.note: The matrix's Fix-a-bug×VS Code row states the same spec is 'the real-socket capture that pinned host.ide.get_diagnostics' wire shape (ide-integration.md follow-up)'; bugfix README confirms the validating room's host.ide.get_diagnostics pull citing docs/proposals/ide-integration.md (a proposal, not an architecture doc).

claims.claim: gh-agent: a bug-labeled issue mention routes to a real dispatch of stories/bugfix in a per-job worktree .worktrees/gh-job-<id>, replaying by default with KITSOKI_GHAGENT_HARNESS=live as explicit opt-in
claims.note: internal/ghagent/router.go maps label "bug" → stories/bugfix; realdispatch.go builds .worktrees/gh-job-<id>; dispatch.go's resolveHarnessMode defaults to replay and only KITSOKI_GHAGENT_HARNESS=live (never credential-sniffed) enables live.

claims.claim: stories/bugfix is the one story with a registered realDispatchPlan today
claims.note: internal/ghagent/realdispatch.go:75 — 'realDispatchPlans is keyed by route.Story. Only stories/bugfix has a plan'.

claims.claim: See ../architecture/github-agent.md#real-dispatch-per-job-worktree for the mechanics and honesty contract
claims.note: docs/architecture/github-agent.md exists with the heading 'Real dispatch: per-job worktree' (anchor real-dispatch-per-job-worktree).

claims.claim: No triage-only route is exposed via a label — a mention always attempts the full pipeline route table in internal/ghagent/router.go
claims.note: router.go's LabelStoryMap has only bug/feature/epic story routes, all full-pipeline (bug → stories/bugfix with judge_mode llm_then_human); no triage label route exists. The matrix corroborates: 'no triage-only label route yet'.

claims.claim: KITSOKI_APP_DIR is still process-global but the racy span (loading each job's app.yaml) is serialized behind a package mutex — see github-agent.md#kitsoki_app_dir-concurrency
claims.note: docs/architecture/github-agent.md has the heading '`KITSOKI_APP_DIR` concurrency' (anchor kitsoki_app_dir-concurrency); the matrix row confirms 'APP_DIR races scoped to the load span'.

claims.claim: Standing proof status: see the fix-bug row of ../testing/dev-workflow-matrix.md
claims.note: docs/testing/dev-workflow-matrix.md exists with a 'Fix a bug' row (🟡 proof-thin across all four surfaces) plus per-surface detail bullets naming docs/workflows/fix-a-bug.md as canonical.

summary: Every actionable claim in docs/workflows/fix-a-bug.md checks out against the tree — files, flows, intents, world keys, CLI shapes, anchors, and gh-agent code all match; no stale references and no missing steps were needed to drive the TUI path.
